# Verifies that 'gs stack submit --fill' triggers an auto-push of the
# integration branch when a prior manual submit has set
# LastPushedHash, but is a no-op when no manual submit has happened.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2025-01-01T00:00:00Z'

cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'

shamhub init
shamhub new origin alice/example.git
shamhub register alice
git push origin main

env SHAMHUB_USERNAME=alice
gs auth login
gs repo init

git add feat-a.txt
gs bc feat-a -m 'Add feat-a'

# Configure integration with feat-a as a tip.
gs integration create preview --tip feat-a
gs integration rebuild

# No manual submit yet: 'gs stack submit --fill' does not push integration.
git checkout main
gs stack submit --fill
! stderr 'Integration branch'

# Verify integration branch is NOT yet on the remote.
cd ../
git clone $SHAMHUB_URL/alice/example.git clone
cd clone
git branch -r
! stdout 'origin/preview'

# Back to repo: manually submit integration.
cd ../repo
gs integration submit
stderr 'Integration branch pushed.'

# Make a follow-up change to feat-a, restack via stack submit.
gs branch checkout feat-a
git add feat-a-extra.txt
gs commit create -m 'feat-a follow-up'

git checkout main
gs stack submit --fill
# Now the auto-submit fires because LastPushedHash is set.
stderr 'Submitting integration branch'

-- repo/feat-a.txt --
feature a
-- repo/feat-a-extra.txt --
extra
